title: OOP Foundations and JavaScript's Object Model

learningObjectives.text: Explain oop principles using a new example and justify the result.
Explain objects and classes as mental models using a new example and justify the result.
learningObjectives.subtopic: Objects and classes as mental models

learningObjectives.text: Explain javascript oop approaches using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: OOP Foundations and JavaScript's Object Model is part of the mental model learners need before making reliable implementation decisions. It belongs to the **Object-Oriented JavaScript** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **OOP principles** → **Objects and classes as mental models** → **JavaScript OOP approaches**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: OOP principles
sections.body: Object-oriented design groups data with behavior and uses ideas such as abstraction, encapsulation, inheritance, and polymorphism as design tools.

sections.title: Objects and classes as mental models
sections.body: A class describes a family of objects; an instance stores state and uses shared behavior. JavaScript can express this through prototypes, classes, factories, or delegation.

sections.title: JavaScript OOP approaches
sections.body: JavaScript supports constructor functions, class syntax, factory functions, and Object.create delegation. Choose a consistent style that communicates ownership and reuse.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.explanation: The object groups progress data with behavior that protects its invariant.

commonMistakes: Memorizing syntax without predicting the underlying value or execution order.
Mutating shared data when the task expects a new value or explicit ownership.
Treating browser APIs and language features as the same layer.
Choosing an abstraction from habit rather than required operations.
summary: OOP Foundations and JavaScript's Object Model connects OOP principles, Objects and classes as mental models, JavaScript OOP approaches.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

questions.prompt: Which description of this design is most accurate?

choices.label: The object has no behavior because factories cannot create methods.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the OOP Foundations and JavaScript's Object Model rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Only inheritance qualifies as OOP.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the OOP Foundations and JavaScript's Object Model rule and the observable result.

choices.label: The factory creates an object that bundles state and behavior; it is a valid object-oriented design even though it does not use class syntax.
choices.feedback: The factory creates an object that bundles state and behavior; it is a valid object-oriented design even though it does not use class syntax.

choices.label: The method stores `score` in a separate global object.

questions.explanation: The factory creates an object that bundles state and behavior; it is a valid object-oriented design even though it does not use class syntax.
questions.hint: Trace the actual values, references, call form, render cycle, request flow, or server/client boundary before choosing.

assessmentProfile.label: Mental model plus practical code reasoning
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic combines an important model with implementation consequences, so both explanation and code reasoning are required.
assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.
